title: Your First Questions for Talk to Data AI
summary: Ask effective first questions after upload—specific, verifiable, grounded in columns you previewed.

content.summary: Ask effective first questions after upload—specific, verifiable, grounded in columns you previewed.

body.text: Talk to Data works best when you treat it like a sharp colleague who still needs clear context from the file you uploaded. Ask totals and rankings tied to named columns: 'Total revenue by region last quarter' after you confirm date and amount columns. Compare AI answers to a bar chart you built manually. Discrepancies usually mean ambiguous column names or unfiltered rows.

body.caption: Calculator and financial data analysis. Photo: Unsplash

body.text: Ask totals and rankings tied to named columns: 'Total revenue by region last quarter' after you confirm date and amount columns.
Compare AI answers to a bar chart you built manually. Discrepancies usually mean ambiguous column names or unfiltered rows.

items: Reference column names visible in preview.
Specify date ranges that match your saved filters.
Avoid vague asks like 'what is interesting here?'

body.text: Once basics match charts, ask comparative questions: which segment grew fastest, which columns correlate with churn flags.
Save filters before complex questions so the AI works on the same subset you intend.
Talk to Data shines when verification is habit—chart first, ask second, reconcile always.

meta_description: Ask effective first questions after upload—specific, verifiable, grounded in columns you previewed.
meta_keywords: Talk to Data, AI, Tutorial, Natural Language
